articles.title: Man sentenced for raping 2 women in Framingham store in 1989
articles.description:
Stephen Paul Gale will likely die in prison, given the sentenced he received more than 30 years after he robbed a Framingham store and raped two employees in 1989.

The post Man sentenced for raping 2 women in Framingham store in 1989 appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Stephen Paul Gale was sentenced for raping two women in a Framingham store in 1989.
Gale's sentence ensures he will likely spend the rest of his life in prison.
This case highlights ongoing efforts to address historical sexual violence in the Boston area.

articles.title: Celtics’ Joe Mazzulla on possibility of winning coach of the year: ‘It’s a stupid award’
articles.description:
"It's more about the players, more about the work that the staff puts in. It's just that simple."

The post Celtics’ Joe Mazzulla on possibility of winning coach of the year: ‘It’s a stupid award’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla downplays Coach of the Year award significance.
Mazzulla emphasizes team effort over individual accolades in recent comments.
Boston fans may appreciate focus on team success rather than personal recognition.

articles.title: 1 dead after crash in Mattapan, police say
articles.description:
1 person was killed in a late night Sunday car crash, police said.

The post 1 dead after crash in Mattapan, police say appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: One person died in a late night car crash in Mattapan on Sunday.
Police reported the incident occurred around 11 PM, details remain under investigation.
Residents are urged to exercise caution on local roads following this tragic event.

articles.title: Should Joe Mazzulla win Coach of the Year? Here’s how he stacks up against the competition.
articles.description:
Mazzulla has the second-best odds to win the award, trailing Detroit's J.B. Bickerstaff.

summary: Joe Mazzulla has the second-best odds for Coach of the Year behind J.B. Bickerstaff.
Mazzulla's performance could boost Celtics' morale and fan support in Boston.
Winning the award may enhance Mazzulla's reputation and future prospects in coaching.

articles.title: Ice sculpture carved for Boston’s ‘No Kings’ rally destroyed by vandals, organizers say
articles.description:
“I designed the sculpture so that if it was vandalized, it would still be the same message, because the vandalizers would literally be ending ICE,” the artist said.

The post Ice sculpture carved for Boston’s ‘No Kings’ rally destroyed by vandals, organizers say appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Vandals destroyed an ice sculpture at Boston's 'No Kings' rally on March 30.
Artist designed the sculpture to convey a message even if vandalized.
The incident highlights ongoing tensions around immigration enforcement in Boston.

articles.title: Worcester man gets 30 years in prison for sexually exploiting minors
articles.description:
Antonio Merced, 29, was sentenced on March 26 by the U.S. District Court Judge Margaret R. Guzman, according to federal prosecutors.

The post Worcester man gets 30 years in prison for sexually exploiting minors appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Antonio Merced, 29, sentenced to 30 years for sexually exploiting minors on March 26.
U.S. District Court Judge Margaret R. Guzman presided over the case in Worcester.
The sentencing aims to deter similar crimes and protect Boston area youth.

articles.title: 4 takeaways from Robert Kraft’s NFL meetings interview
articles.description:
Kraft touched on expectations for the 2026 Patriots, an 18th regular-season game, and more.

The post 4 takeaways from Robert Kraft’s NFL meetings interview appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Robert Kraft discussed expectations for the 2026 Patriots season during NFL meetings.
Kraft supports adding an 18th regular-season game, impacting scheduling for Boston fans.
Changes could affect ticket prices and game attendance for local Patriots supporters.

articles.title: Jayson Tatum earns his first Eastern Conference player of the week award of the season
articles.description:
Tatum trails only Paul Pierce and Larry Bird on the franchise list.

The post Jayson Tatum earns his first Eastern Conference player of the week award of the season appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jayson Tatum named Eastern Conference Player of the Week for the first time this season.
Tatum ranks third in Celtics history, behind Paul Pierce and Larry Bird.
His performance boosts Celtics' morale and strengthens their playoff positioning.
